rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LAWRENCE LEVENE VS. BONDEX INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 2, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Volkswagen Group of America Inc.¿s motion for pro hac vice admission of Michael B. Sena is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Amend The Complaint To Cure Improper Defendant Name Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Amend The Complaint To Add Doe Defendant
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 2, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to amend the complaint is cure improper defendant name and alternative motion to amend the complaint to add Doe defendant: Plaintiffs¿ motion to amend the complaint to cure improper defendant name pursuant to C.C.P. section 473(a) is denied. Plaintiffs¿ motion to amend the complaint to substitute Cahill Construction Co. for Doe defendant is granted pursuant to C.C.P. section 474. Plaintiffs have 10 days leave to amend the complaint. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY ROBERTSON et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 2, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a) and 1010.6 and CRC 2.251. Plaintiffs¿ objection based on untimeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 2, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. rulings.Case Title: LARRY ROBERTSON et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 2, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages: On July 31, 2012, this Court issued an order to stay as to Defendant Kaiser Gypsum, pending a disqualification motion. As a result of the order the Court cannot hear Defendant's motion for summary adjudication until the stay is lifted. The motion for disqualification of plaintiffs' attorney is presently set for hearing on August 20, 2012. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is continued to August 20, 2012. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY ROBERTSON et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 2, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Cyprus Amax Minerals Company¿s motion for summary judgment: plaintiffs¿ request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. section 437c(h) is granted. Plaintiffs demonstrated that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot now be presented. A hearing is required to discuss supplemental briefing and the new hearing date. The hearing will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016.
